issues.issue: The citation for Chiri is missing. It is mentioned in the prose but not linked to a reference.
issues.quote: Kindaichi and Chiri <Ref k="kindaichi1936" /> through the modern reference grammars of Tamura <Ref k="tamura1996" />; <Ref k="tamura2000" />, Refsing <Ref k="refsing1986" />, Shibatani <Ref k="shibatani1990" />, Satō <Ref k="sato2008" />, Bugaeva <Ref k="bugaeva2022" />, and Nakagawa <Ref k="nakagawa2024" />.
issues.suggestion: Add a citation for Chiri, likely <Ref k="chiri1942" /> based on the table later in the chapter.

issues.issue: The text names Chiri alongside Kindaichi but only cites Kindaichi 1936. The table lists Chiri 1942 as a separate defining work, so Chiri should have its own citation here.
issues.quote: drawing on the full range of prior scholarship from Kindaichi and Chiri <Ref k="kindaichi1936" />
issues.suggestion: Add a separate <Ref k="chiri1942" /> after Kindaichi's reference, or cite both jointly.

issues.issue: Chiri is mentioned but not cited with a reference key; only Kindaichi1936 is cited here, creating a citation mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Add <Ref k="chiri1942" /> after Chiri

issues.issue: The prose mentions Tamura's Saru dictionary and grammar monograph, but only cites the dictionary (<Ref k="tamura1996" />) and then the grammar monograph (<Ref k="tamura2000" />) separately. It might be clearer to link both to the description of the Saru dialect.
issues.suggestion: Clarify the relationship between the two Tamura citations and the description of the Saru dialect.

issues.issue: The semicolon-separated citations are not formatted consistently with the rest of the text; typically, multiple citations are separated by commas or listed in a single <Ref> tag if supported. This may be a formatting error.
issues.suggestion: Use a single <Ref> tag with multiple keys if supported, or separate with commas

issues.issue: The prose mentions Kindaichi and Chiri together as establishing basic morphological architecture, but Chiri is not cited here, only Kindaichi.
issues.suggestion: Add a citation for Chiri here as well.

issues.issue: The citation for Kindaichi 1936 is repeated at the end of the sentence, but it was already cited earlier in the paragraph. This creates redundant citation markup.
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate <Ref k="kindaichi1936" />

issues.issue: The citation key is 'bugaeva2012', but the work listed in the table and referenced in the prose is from 2022. This appears to be a typo in the citation key, as Bugaeva's handbook is from 2022, not 2012.
issues.suggestion: Change citation key from 'bugaeva2012' to 'bugaeva2022'

issues.issue: Placeholders 'XX–XXI' have not been resolved to actual part numbers. This is a production-task remnant that should not appear in the final text.
issues.quote: the diachrony and contact parts of the grammar (Parts XX–XXI)
issues.suggestion: Replace 'Parts XX–XXI' with the actual part numbers (e.g., Parts 20–21) or with the part titles if numbering is not yet fixed.

issues.issue: The narrative discusses only a subset of the works listed in the preceding table. Bugaeva (2012) is in the table but is never mentioned in the text; Chiri (1942) is in the table but receives only a passing name reference without a separate citation or discussion. The text claims to review 'each work' but omits two of them.
issues.quote: Each work contributes something indispensable. Kindaichi and Chiri establish the basic morphological architecture […] Refsing provides the most systematic morphosyntactic account […] Shibatani places Ainu within a typological account […] Tamura's Saru dictionary and grammar monograph remain the central data source […] Satō's grammar gives the most thorough treatment […] The Bugaeva Handbook supplies extensive English coverage […] Nakagawa's grammar brings the description of Hokkaido Ainu to its greatest single-volume comprehensiveness
issues.suggestion: Either add a sentence or two about Bugaeva (2012) and give Chiri (1942) its own citation and description, or remove those entries from the table if they are not considered defining for this grammar's position.

issues.issue: The number of 'leaf chapters' (176) seems very high for a grammar, especially when compared to the benchmark of Nakagawa (2024) which has 28 top-level chapters. This might be a typo or a misunderstanding of 'leaf chapters'.
issues.quote: distributing 176 leaf chapters across 23 structural parts
issues.suggestion: Verify the number 176. If it refers to individual sections within larger parts, clarify the terminology.

issues.issue: The phrase 'flagged for editorial review' addresses the internal production process rather than the reader, potentially confusing the reader about the status of the text.
issues.quote: this grade is restricted to the five formal domains listed in the previous section and is flagged for editorial review
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to describe the grade's function without referencing the editorial workflow, e.g., 'marks new analyses that are presented as proposals for the reader's evaluation'.
